prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response:
"El Cant dels Ocells" by Pau Casals

This haunting Catalan song, played by the cello maestro Pau Casals, is a powerful evocation of longing and melancholy. According to legend, when Casals played this in a refugee camp during the Spanish Civil War, it moved those listening to tears. Its simple melody, played with mournful resonance, seems to tap into a deep well of human sorrow. There's an ineffable power to music that speaks directly to the soul, transcending language and time. In "El Cant dels Ocells," I hear the echoes of centuries of human experience, distilled into a few haunting notes.
